Final CSV: five workloads x two Agent ratios x five client levels. Speedups use the best available baseline at the same workload, ratio, and client count.
Smooth scaling and zero aborts, but 0.98-1.00x means no ATCC throughput gain.
Agent TPS improves, but total/background TPS falls as clients increase; do not claim system-wide scaling.
Near baseline parity; does not support the paper's order-of-magnitude medium-contention claim.
Do not use in main figure
Mixed wins/losses, high CV at 8 clients, and unstable P99/total-throughput curves.
Curve saturates smoothly, but speedup is only 0.92-1.28x and abort reaches 30.8%.
Do not use in main figure
Large ratios rely on near-zero baselines; 24->32 has a 94% Agent-TPS jump and total/background TPS cliffs.
Monotone scaling shape, but 5x clients yields 3.62x TPS and every point is slightly below the best baseline.
Do not use in main figure
Agent TPS scales to 32 clients, then falls; only one point clearly beats the best baseline and total TPS is irregular.
Strong 1.44-3.91x Agent-TPS and large P99 gains, but abort is 27-76% and TPS declines after saturation at 16 clients.
Do not use in main figure
Four speedups are undefined because baseline Agent TPS is zero; abort is 40-47% and total/background TPS collapses.

values: The current matrix contains OCC, 2PL-wait-die, Silo, and Polaris. It cannot substantiate exact comparisons against MOCC, Polyjuice, or Wound-Wait.
The current matrix contains OCC, 2PL-wait-die, Silo, and Polaris. It cannot substantiate exact comparisons against MOCC, Polyjuice, or Wound-Wait.

The current matrix contains OCC, 2PL-wait-die, Silo, and Polaris. It cannot substantiate exact comparisons against MOCC, Polyjuice, or Wound-Wait.
The current matrix contains OCC, 2PL-wait-die, Silo, and Polaris. It cannot substantiate exact comparisons against MOCC, Polyjuice, or Wound-Wait.
YCSB medium: 18.1x vs Polaris, 76.9x vs Polyjuice; ~16% fewer tokens; 5x lower tail latency
All-Agent speedup 0.95-0.99x; 8:2 speedup 0.92-1.92x with unstable P99.
Magnitude is far below the paper and Polyjuice is absent.
ATCC is near baseline parity in the current medium-contention implementation.
Do not quote the paper's medium-contention multipliers for this system.
YCSB high: 3.1x vs MOCC, 2.2x vs Wound-Wait; abort <30%; much lower tokens and tail latency
All-Agent speedup 0.92-1.28x and abort 17.6-30.8%. 8:2 ratios are 17.5-28.6x but several baselines are near zero.
Missing named baselines; denominator-floor inflation; 8:2 total/background cliffs.
The 8:2 ATCC Agent path remains productive under severe contention, with an explicit denominator caveat.
Do not present 17-29x as a robust algorithmic speedup or claim abort <30% at every client count.
All-Agent TPS rises 25.5 -> 92.3 while clients rise 8 -> 40 (3.62x TPS for 5x clients).
Scaling is monotone but sublinear and slightly below the best baseline at all points.
TPC-C low scales monotonically through 40 clients.
Do not call the observed curve near-linear without a quantified efficiency caveat.
TPC-C high: 2.2x vs Wound-Wait and >10x vs other methods
All-Agent Agent TPS is 1.44-3.91x vs the best available baseline; 3/5 points exceed 2.2x. P99 reduction is large.
Wound-Wait is absent; speedup drops below 2.2x at 32/40; abort reaches 75.9%.
ATCC improves Agent TPS by 1.44-3.91x and materially lowers P99 versus the tested best baseline.
Do not claim >10x, Wound-Wait superiority, or controlled abort at high concurrency.
TPC-C high All-Agent P99 reduction is approximately 67-88% versus the best Agent-TPS baseline.
The result is workload-specific and uses one-seed formal overrides.
Up to 88% P99 reduction on TPC-C high in this matrix.
Do not generalize the reduction to all workloads or claim a multi-seed confidence interval.
